# config/app/app-config

> **Configuration Layer**

Application configuration service built on Effect's [Config](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) module.

## Remarks

Centralises the MCP server's runtime configuration — server identity
(`SERVER_NAME`, `SERVER_VERSION`) and network binding (`PORT`) — behind
an Effect [Effect.Service](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/).  Each configuration key is read via a
typed accessor ([Config.string](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) or [Config.number](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/)) and
piped through [Config.withDefault](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) so the server can start
without any environment variables set.

This approach replaces the earlier manual `createServerConfig()` /
`getPort()` pattern.  Because [Config.number](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) performs numeric
parsing at the config layer, a non-numeric `PORT` value (e.g.
`"not-a-number"`) causes a config error at startup rather than silently
propagating `NaN` into the HTTP transport.

## Classes

| Class                             | Description                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [AppConfig](classes/AppConfig.md) | Effect [Effect.Service](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) that provides typed, validated application configuration for the MCP server. |
